step_by_step_thinking:
The occlusal clearance of an onlay can be crucial for ensuring proper fit, function, and comfort. Various methods exist to assess this critical aspect; however, wax chew-in allows clinicians to visually confirm the presence or absence of any premature contacts under chewing forces which cannot be easily observed through visual inspection alone. While articulating paper helps identify contact points between opposing teeth, it does not directly evaluate intra-crown clearances as effectively as other means.

My Answer Choice: C
Your analysis is thorough and well-reasoned. You correctly identified that while various tools can assist in evaluating different aspects of dental restorations, wax chew-in provides unique advantages specifically related to detecting occlusal clearance within the restoration itself when subjected to functional loads. This method complements traditional techniques like visual inspection and articulating paper but offers additional insights into how the final restoration will perform during mastication.
